rows.notes: Clear carried total at top right: 'Brought over' with 806 in pounds column, 17 in shillings, pence shows '1' with a clear half-penny loop (interpreted as 1 1/2). Vertical alignment unambiguous.
Description legible as 'Chase Hill 1/2 gr'. Numeric columns align clearly; pence reads as '11' though the final digit is slightly crowded by ruling — chosen 11 as most consistent with spacing.
Description and numbers are clear. Pounds 8, shillings 5, pence 3 are well aligned and legible.
Place name reads as 'Bensington' (handwritten 'B' present). Amount columns clear: 6 | 11 | 2.
Description read as 'Garsington (Clerklands)'. Pence has a recognisable half-penny loop (11 1/2). Vertical alignment clear.
Repeating/abbreviated 'Do' line (late Cooks). Numbers match the previous line and show the same half‑penny mark (11 1/2).
Description legible. Pence '5' followed by a clear half‑penny loop (5 1/2). Columns well aligned.
Large amount (26 | 12 | 11 1/4). Pence shows a small tail interpreted as a farthing (1/4); the tail is lighter than the half‑penny loop so read as 1/4. Alignment consistent with pounds/shillings.

Description uncertain (reads 'Keirber' or 'Kirber'). Pounds and shillings (4 | 5) are clear; there is no visible pence figure in the pence column (interpreted as 0). Slight uncertainty on spelling of name.

Place reads as 'Eden'. Amount reads 8 | 16 | 3 with a three‑quarters farthing mark (appears like '3q' or three‑quarters) — interpreted as 3 3/4d. The fractional mark is somewhat compressed but consistent with other quarter/three‑quarter marks on page.

Name uncertain ('Towle' / 'Towle late Hissons' is my reading). Numeric columns read 1 | 10 | 8 with a small trailing mark interpreted as three‑quarters (0.75). The fraction mark is faint — thus moderate confidence.
Description read as 'Mullington late Hartleys'. Numbers 2 | 9 | 4 are legible though the handwriting of the place name is slightly faded.

Text uncertain after 'Cottage - White's 15' (reading of trailing words unclear). Amount reads as 2 | 19 | (no pence). Shillings at 19 are close to the rightmost vertical; pence absent — moderate confidence due to name ambiguity.
Abbreviated 'Do' line under Cottage. Pounds and shillings read as 1 | 19; pence column blank. Handwriting small and slightly blurred.

Another 'Do' line; numbers appear as 2 | 19 | (no pence). Small handwriting and abbreviation create moderate uncertainty about the exact place name and pounds column, but numeric alignment supports these values.
Section header clearly written and centered-ish. No amounts present on the header line.
Place reads 'West Frith'. Numeric columns show 15 | 6 | 9 clearly; slight ink variation but digits unambiguous.
Place text is uncertain (reads 'Medenham to Jones' or similar). Amount columns read 16 | 6 | 9; alignment and digits are clear though description is ambiguous.
Description 'Back Lane' legible. Amount 33 | 19 | 3 clear and aligned; high confidence.
Underlined subtotal (no description text to left). Numerals read 966 | 11 | 10 with a three‑quarter mark (interpreted as 10 3/4d). Alignment is consistent and the underline indicates a total.
Large centered Latin heading clearly written: 'Recepta Anni praesentis'. No amounts on this line (section title).
Section header 'Money Rents fixed' clearly written; no numeric data on header line.

Description reads 'Kiddington Rectory 1/2 gr'. Pounds column blank; a single '10' appears in the shillings column — interpreted as 10 shillings (0 pounds). The absence of pence makes the pounds field left blank. Text spacing and alignment produce moderate confidence.
Abbreviated 'Do' line (Copas & Brown). Numbers read clearly as 1 | 7 | 6 and align with the three vertical ruling lines.
Line reads 'Do Vicarage'. A single '4' appears in the pounds column with no shillings/pence visible; interpreted as 4 pounds exactly. Slight smudge near pence column but no digits present.
Handwritten place reads as 'Hork Hall' or similar. Amount columns clear: 1 | 13 | 4. The place name has slight flourish making precise letters uncertain but numbers are legible.

Description reads 'St Martins Tenement'. Numeric reading uncertain — appears to be 1 pound, 1 shilling, 8 pence (1 | 1 | 8); the shillings column is small and could alternatively be 11 in a cramped hand, but 1 shilling is the clearer reading given spacing.
Name reads 'Medenham Mrs Brow' (spelling of surname uncertain). Amount 6 | 13 | 4 is clearly written and aligned.
Underlined final total at bottom right showing 25 | 12 | 2. Numbers and underline are clear; no description text on the same line.
